Heatwave warning for 7 districts in Rajasthan:Rain in Sriganganagar; Udaipur zoo serves fruit ice cream to bears
- Copy link
The heatwave conditions across Rajasthan are expected to intensify from May 24, with the Meteorological Department issuing orange and yellow alerts for several districts. While maximum temperatures in all cities remained below 45 degrees Celsius on Friday, many districts continued to experience scorching heat and warm nights.
Bikaner, Churu, Phalodi and Chittorgarh recorded some of the highest temperatures in the state. Meanwhile, light rain in Sriganganagar on Saturday morning brought temporary relief from the intense heat.
Heatwave likely to worsen in coming days
According to weather experts, temperatures may rise by 2 to 3 degrees Celsius over the next few days. Severe heatwave conditions are likely in western Rajasthan, while eastern and northern districts may witness heatwave along with warm night conditions.
The Meteorological Department has issued a yellow alert for seven districts on May 23 and an orange alert from May 24 onwards.
Rain brings relief in Sriganganagar
The weather changed suddenly in Sriganganagar district on Saturday morning around 6.30 am, followed by light rainfall in the city and nearby rural areas. The rain caused a drop in temperature and provided relief from the heatwave conditions that had continued for the last four to five days.
Chittorgarh records highest temperature
During the last 24 hours, Chittorgarh recorded the highest maximum temperature at 44.2 degrees Celsius. Heatwave conditions were also reported from Phalodi, Churu, Bikaner and Ganganagar. In eastern Rajasthan, districts such as Tonk, Alwar and Dausa experienced strong hot winds.
District-wise weather report
Jaipur
Jaipur continued to witness intense heat and strong hot winds on Friday. The maximum temperature was recorded at 41.5 degrees Celsius, while the minimum temperature stood at 30.7 degrees Celsius. The city also experienced warm night conditions. The Meteorological Department has predicted severe heatwave conditions in Jaipur for the next three to four days.
Jodhpur
Jodhpur recorded a maximum temperature of 42 degrees Celsius, which was 0.8 degrees above normal. The minimum temperature settled at 29.4 degrees Celsius, 1.3 degrees above normal. Due to extreme heat and harsh sunlight, movement in markets and on roads reduced during afternoon hours.
Udaipur
Temperatures have been rising continuously in Udaipur for the last two days. On May 22, the city recorded a maximum temperature of 41 degrees Celsius and a minimum of 24.2 degrees Celsius. Residents remained troubled due to strong sunlight and rising heat during daytime.
Alwar
Alwar recorded a maximum temperature of 43.1 degrees Celsius on Friday, while the minimum temperature remained at 30.5 degrees Celsius. To provide relief from the heat, water was sprinkled on roads in several areas of the city.
Ajmer
Ajmer recorded a maximum temperature of 40.9 degrees Celsius and a minimum of 29.3 degrees Celsius. The Meteorological Department said the weather is expected to remain mostly clear during the coming week. Dusty winds with speeds of 20 to 30 kmph may bring a slight drop in temperature over the next 48 hours.
Kota
Kota remained among the hottest cities, recording a maximum temperature of 46.3 degrees Celsius. The minimum temperature was recorded at 29.1 degrees Celsius. The intense heat also affected wildlife, with animals seen searching for shade during daytime.
Sikar
Sikar and nearby areas have been facing severe heat for the last six days. On Friday, the maximum temperature was recorded at 40 degrees Celsius and the minimum at 28 degrees Celsius. Due to the scorching heat and hot winds, activity in markets and on roads remained low during afternoon hours.

title: Rajasthan Weather Today [23 May, 2026]: Check IMD Rain Predictions, Heat Alert & Thunderstorms Across Jaipur, Ajmer, Jodhpur, Alwar, Kota, Bikaner, Udaipur, Jaisalmer & More — Full City-Wise Forecast - The Sunday Guardian
full_text:
Rajasthan remains under severe heatwave conditions as temperatures approach 45°C across multiple districts. IMD alerts continue till May 28 with limited rain chances.
Rajasthan Weather Today: Rajasthan continues to battle one of the harshest heat spells of the season, with temperatures in several districts nearing or crossing the 45°C mark. The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has extended heatwave alerts across large parts of northwest India until May 28, warning that dry weather and scorching winds could intensify over the coming days. Cities across western and northern Rajasthan remain under severe thermal stress, while isolated cloud activity and light rain possibilities offer little immediate relief while Sri Ganganagar, Churu, Pilani and Bikaner remain among the hottest locations in the state.
The IMD has issued heatwave and severe heatwave warnings for multiple states including Rajasthan, Haryana, Uttar Pradesh, Madhya Pradesh, Bihar and Odisha.

The weather department has also warned that hot nighttime temperatures may worsen health risks, especially for children, elderly residents and outdoor workers.
Western Rajasthan is likely to witness temperatures above 45°C in isolated districts over the next four days. Dry desert winds and low humidity levels are accelerating daytime heating.

The “feels-like” temperature in urban centers has risen even higher because of heat retention from concrete structures and reduced nighttime cooling.
Air quality across several Rajasthan cities has worsened due to dust movement, dry winds and intense sunlight.
Visibility levels in some districts dropped below 3 miles because of suspended dust particles and haze conditions.
Health authorities have advised residents to avoid outdoor exposure between 12 PM and 4 PM, when temperatures remain at peak levels.
